title: Scabies contact documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports documenting close skin contact, household context, exposure before symptoms, characteristic distribution, and clinician-completed visual, dermoscopic, or microscopy assessment without inferring diagnosis.
assessment.rationale: The exact WHO transmission and diagnosis section supports documenting a scabies-contact context, onset, relevant contact and household history, skin inspection and distribution without asserting infestation.

source.title: Scabies
source.population: People with possible scabies or close contact with an affected person, including children, older people, and immunosuppressed people.
source.setting: Community, primary care, outbreak, institutional, and dermatology assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact for contact context, symptom timing, itch, burrows, papules, distribution, pediatric patterns, immunosuppression, crusted disease, and secondary infection documentation. It is not used to diagnose scabies or generate treatment or contact instructions.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting close skin contact, household context, exposure before symptoms, characteristic distribution, and clinician-completed visual, dermoscopic, or microscopy assessment without inferring diagnosis.

assessment.text: Supports documenting itch, night worsening, burrows, papules, site distribution, infant or child distribution, immunosuppression, crusting, and secondary skin infection context.
assessment.rationale: The exact symptom and population section supports recording itch, burrows, papules, pediatric distribution, immunosuppression, crusting and secondary infection only when assessed.
